markdownDescription: Specifies the fields to use for pre-sorting the index. Pre-sorting improves query performance when using this exact specification in `$search.sort`. You can specify a single field or multiple fields for a compound sort. You can specify 1 for ascending, -1 for descending, or an object with `order` and `noData`. To learn more, see [Atlas Search Index Sort Options](https://dochub.mongodb.org/core/atlas-search-sort).
description: Specifies the fields to use for pre-sorting the index.

oneOf.markdownDescription: Sort order for this field. Use `1` for ascending order or `-1` for descending order.

order.markdownDescription: Sort order for this field. Use `1` for ascending order or `-1` for descending order.

noData.markdownDescription: Specifies how to sort documents that are missing this field. Use `lowest` to sort missing values first or `highest` to sort them last. Defaults to `lowest`.
noData.description: How to sort documents missing this field: 'lowest' or 'highest'.
